title: TCP Connections, Accept Loops, Half-Closes, Deadlines, and Failure · Go Scenario Checkpoint
summary: A security review compares near-miss parser decisions for tcp connections, accept loops, half-closes, deadlines, and failure and requires the smallest strict bounded TCP connection lifecycle state machine with changed-input evidence. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for bounded TCP connection lifecycle state machine. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Explain ordered reliable byte-stream delivery while accounting for segmentation, retransmission, flow control, congestion, and missing message boundaries.
Assign listener, accepted connection, goroutine, buffer, deadline, and close ownership with bounded accept-error handling.
Set and refresh absolute read and write deadlines by protocol phase and classify timeout evidence without assuming cancellation.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Write Ambiguity Retry
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz protocol team handles case 9379 in a partner TLS endpoint negotiating HTTP/1.1 and HTTP/2 through ALPN. Build deterministic Go evidence to classify connection, deadline, completion, and close state without inventing message boundaries; preserve this operating constraint: diagnostics must omit credentials and attacker-controlled metric labels; then reject a protocol transition while optimistic bytes are buffered and prevent desynchronization. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Treat failed or timed-out writes as potentially partially observed and avoid blind replay of non-idempotent protocol effects.

The visible result looks correct, but its invariant or value contract is unverified. What must happen next?

Evidence target: Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Write Ambiguity Retry.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for treat failed or timed-out writes as potentially partially observed and avoid blind replay of non-idempotent protocol effects. For Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Write Ambiguity Retry, keep Write Ambiguity Retry connected to executable Go behavior evidence.
